# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Docker Compose configuration for ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana). Define services for log collection, storage, and visualization. Configure Logstash pipelines for log parsing. Expose Kibana UI and Elasticsearch ports for access. This is for our centralized logging platform.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for elasticsearch logs exposed and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: elasticsearch_logs_exposed


version: '2'  # Define Docker Compose Version (default is v3)
services:   # Start defining services here for ELK stack
    elasticsearch:     # Name of service, can be any name you want to use. Can also include version if needed e.g., "elasticsearch:7" or even a tag like so -> 'elasticsearch:latest'  (default is latest)  
        image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch    # Docker Image URL, replace with your preferred ElasticSearch Version and Platform if needed e.g., "docker.elastic.co/kibana" or even a tag like so -> 'latest'  (default is latest)
        ports:   # Define the exposed port for each service here as per requirement ie Logstash, Kibana etc.. can be done in this way e.g., "ports": ["5042:5043", "9201:9201"]  (default is none)
            - '9200:9200'   # Expose ElasticSearch port for access, can be done in this way e.g., "- 80" or even a tag like so '- latest' to make it default ie "5671->latest". (default is none)
            - '5043:5043'   # Expose ElasticSearch port for access, can be done in this way e.g., "- 80" or even a tag like so '- latest'.(default is none). Can also include multiple ports here separated by comma as per requirement ie "ports": ["9201:5673", "4567->latest"]. (default is none)
        environment:   # Define the env variables for ElasticSearch, can be done in this way e.g., “environment” : { 'ES_JAVA_OPTS' - '-Xms1g', 'ELASTIC_HOME': '/usr/share/elasticsearch'} (default is none)
            ES_JAVA_OPTS: '-Xms2000m'   # Java Options for ElasticSearch, can be done in this way e.g., "ES_MEMLOCK" - '512m'.(Defaults to 4GB if not provided). (default is none)
            ELASTIC_HOME: '/usr/share/elasticsearch'   # Path of elastic search home directory, can be done in this way e.g., "ELASTICSEARCH" - '"/opt/docker-elasticsearch"'(Defaults to /var/lib/docker if not provided). (default is none)
        volumes:    # Define the data volume for ElasticSearch here as per requirement ie Logstash, Kibana etc.. can be done in this way e.g., "volumes": { '/data' : {} }  or even a tag like so -> 'latest'. (default is none)
            /var/lib/docker:/data   # Data volume for ElasticSearch here as per requirement ie Logstash, Kibana etc.. can be done in this way e.g., "/usr/share/logstash" : {}  or even a tag like so -> 'latest'. (default is none)
        networks:    # Define the network for ElasticSearch here as per requirement ie Logstash, Kibana etc.. can be done in this way e.g., "networks": { default_bridge : {} }  or even a tag like so -> 'latest'. (default is none)
            elastic-network   # Network name for ElasticSearch here as per requirement ie Logstash, Kibana etc.. can be done in this way e.g., "elastic" .(Defaults to default if not provided).  or even a tag like so -> 'latest'. (default is none)
    logstash:   # Name of service for the same as elasticsearch above but with different image and ports, can be done in this way e.g., "logstash" : {image:"docker-registry/my_customized_logstash",ports:[5042]}. (default is none)
        depends_on:   # Define the services that should start before starting LogStash here as per requirement ie ElasticSearch, Kibana etc.. can be done in this way e.g., "depends_on": ["elasticsearch", “kibana”]. (default is none)
            elasticsearch:   # Services to wait for if not started before starting LogStash here as per requirement ie ElasticSearch, Kibana etc.. can be done in this way e.g., "depends_on": ["elastic-network", “logstash”]. (default is none)
        image: dockerregistry/mycustomizedLogstash   # Docker Image URL for LogStash here as per requirement ie ElasticSearch, Kibana etc.. can be done in this way e.g., "image": 'docker-logstash' or even a tag like so -> latest'. (default is none)
        ports:    # Define the exposed port for each service there and same as elasticsearch above but with different image, depends_on etc.. can be done in this way e.g., "ports": ["5043"]  or even a tag like so -> 'latest'. (default is none)
        volumes:   # Define the data volume for LogStash here as per requirement ie ElasticSearch, Kibana etc.. can be done in this way e.g., "volumes": { '/var/logstash' : {} }  or even a tag like so -> 'latest'. (default is none)
        networks:   # Define the network for LogStash here as per requirement ie ElasticSearch, Kibana etc.. can be done in this way e.g., "networks": { default_bridge : {} }  or even a tag like so -> 'latest'. (default is none)
            elastic-network   # Network name for LogStash here as per requirement ie ElasticSearch, Kibana etc.. can be done in this way e.g., "elastic" .(Defaults to default if not provided).  or even a tag like so -> 'latest'. (default is none)
    kibana:   # Name of service for the same as elasticsearch above but with different image and ports, can be done in this way e.g., "kibana" : {image:"docker-registry/my_customized_KIBANA",ports:[5043]}. (default is none)
        depends_on:   # Define the services that should start before starting Kibana here as per requirement ie ElasticSearch, Logstash etc.. can be done in this way e.g., "depends_on": ["elasticsearch", “logstash”]. (default is none)
            elasticsearch:   # Services to wait for if not started before starting Kibana here as per requirement ie ElasticSearch, LogStash etc.. can be done in this way e.g., "depends
